tests_added_red_first: test_abstention_is_scored_but_failure_is_missing (success-None scored 0 + persisted; raise -> MISSING, n_errors++, not a scored abstention)
test_failure_recalled_on_resume_excluded_from_answered_set (missing cells absent from the answered set; re-called on resume; zero re-spend elsewhere)
test_auto_resume_reuses_preserved_214_cells (the REAL preserved checkpoint: 214 non-None re-used, re-call ONLY the missing)
test_run_auto_resumes_with_no_flag_zero_respend (run()-level: relaunch with NO flag -> 0 stub calls)
test_atomic_write_json_round_trips (temp+os.replace; no leftover .tmp as live path)
test_atomic_checkpoint_survives_midwrite_death (mid-serialize death -> prior checkpoint intact, never half-written)
tdd_evidence.red_was_7_of_8_failing: test_auto_resume_reuses_preserved_214_cells passed at RED because the non-None reuse mechanism already existed at the baseline; it documents the contract. The other 7 were RED.
tdd_evidence.tests_green: 28 (test_m1_verdict.py 24 + test_m1_answerer_retry.py 4) on merged main; 0 skipped on canonical (preserved checkpoint present).
tdd_evidence.kept_green: all pre-existing test_m1_verdict.py + test_m1_answerer_retry.py tests unchanged-and-green; test_m1_baseline.py green (run_baseline._do change is behind the exception path only).
tdd_evidence.pyright: 0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 informations on m1_verdict.py + m1_verdict_run.py (and m1_baseline.py)

three_improvements_confirmed.1_auto_resume_default: run() auto-detects <output>.checkpoint.json via _resolve_resume (explicit --resume is an override/alternate source) and loads it WITHOUT a flag, reusing every already-answered (qid,arm) cell and re-calling only the missing/failed; run_baseline now does a FINAL unconditional checkpoint flush so the sidecar always reflects the finished pass (no tail-loss). Any restart (eviction/manual/crash) continues with zero re-spend, no restart-from-scratch.
three_improvements_confirmed.2_failure_not_abstention: m1_baseline._do split: a SUCCESSFUL None = a legit abstention (persisted, scored F1=0, NOT retried); a retry-exhausted raise (429/5xx/timeout after backoff) = a FAILURE -> cell left MISSING (never written, excluded from scoring + the checkpoint's answered set, re-called on resume, counted in n_errors -> answer_completeness). A failed cell is NEVER persisted as a scored F1=0 abstention (the silent per-arm deflation bug). _bump_n_errors is lock-safe under answer_workers>1.
three_improvements_confirmed.3_atomic_frequent_checkpoint: _atomic_write_json = temp-file + os.replace (atomic rename, same fs) -> a mid-write death can only leave a discardable .tmp; the live checkpoint is never half-written. Cadence default 25 -> 10 questions; answer_completeness INVALID-below-floor guard kept, now fed by the failure/abstention split (n_errors = missing failures, not deflating abstentions).

resume_smoke_result.command: run(mode=priced, REAL corpus + REAL extractions, sidecar = copy of data/corpus-data/0.8.2-m1-verdict.checkpoint.json, STUB answerer + FakeEncoder/FakeReranker)
resume_smoke_result.assertion: stub re-calls == expected_cells(1500) - reused(214) == 1286 -> re-called ONLY the missing cells; divergence guard passed (66/300, fraction 0.22)

src/python/eval/m1_verdict_run.py (_atomic_write_json; _resolve_resume; auto-resume default in run(); injection seams answerer/encoder/reranker/questions/extractions; checkpoint_every 25->10; INVALID-guard text updated to failure=missing)

next_step_for_orchestrator: resilient harness merged; orchestrator launches the priced pass auto-resuming from data/corpus-data/0.8.2-m1-verdict.checkpoint.json -> no re-spend. Point --output at the canonical run path and copy/move the preserved 214-cell checkpoint to <output>.checkpoint.json (or pass it via --resume) so run() auto-detects it; failures now re-call (not deflate) and a kill is resumable with zero re-spend until answer_completeness clears the 0.97 floor.
